title: Optional parameters should be avoided
description: The overloading mechanism should be used in place of optional parameters for several reasons: Optional parameter values are baked into the method call site code, thus, if a default value has been changed, all referencing assemblies need to be rebuilt, otherwise the original values will be used. The Common Language Specification (CLS) allows compilers to ignore default parameter values, and thus require the caller to explicitly specify the values. For example, if you want to consume a method with default argument from another .NET compatible language (for instance C++/CLI), you will have to provide all arguments.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Method has optional parameters - consider using method overloads instead

logic.detection_conditions: Uses regex to detect parameter assignments (optional parameters) in method declarations
logic.triggering_conditions: Method declarations with parameters that have default values (pattern: param = value)
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes test methods and logging contexts
logic.reasoning: Detects optional parameters in method declarations by looking for parameter assignment patterns (param = value). Optional parameters should be replaced with method overloads for better CLS compliance and versioning behavior.

should_trigger: public void Configure(string name, bool enabled = true, int timeout = 30)
